column1: Summary of where every Garage Scholars service sits relative to the Denver/national market.
OVERALL VERDICT: Your pricing is WITHIN MARKET across all service lines. No service is priced above market ceiling.

GARAGE ORG (Undergrad / Graduate / Doctorate): All three sit in the LOW-to-MID range of market pricing. Strong value props with bundled haul-away, bins, and deep clean that most competitors charge extra for.

GYM INSTALL (Warm Up / Super Set): These two are ABOVE pure assembly pricing ($75-$330/piece on Thumbtack). However, you're offering a full-crew white-glove service, not a solo handyman with a wrench. Position these as "gym setup service" not "equipment assembly" in marketing.

1 REP MAX: BEST VALUE PLAY in your lineup. At $4,797 with flooring + platform + wall storage + 5-piece assembly, you massively undercut the $7K-$25K range for home gym builds. This is your strongest selling point.

COMBO PACKAGES: Both Dean's List ($6,497) and Valedictorian ($7,997) price well below what clients would pay hiring separate contractors for equivalent work ($5K-$15K+). The one-vendor convenience is a major differentiator.

DENVER MARKET GAP: You fill a space between budget Thumbtack organizers ($500-$2,500) and premium garage renovation companies ($10K-$25K+). Nobody else in Denver bundles garage org + gym install + haul-away + flooring as a single service.

RISK AREAS: Warm Up and Super Set are the only services where price sensitivity could be an issue. Consider lead-magnet pricing or bundling them with higher-margin garage org tiers to reduce standalone sticker shock.
